_meta.purpose: Learning register for the AI PM content track. One topic per month, anchored at the 21st LinkedIn slot. Spec: analysis/distribution/DISTRIBUTION_SPEC.md §8.
_meta.admission_rule: No entry is marked published without at least one measurement carrying a value AND a source file. Prose alone does not qualify.
_meta.capture_rule: When any session produces a number that measures the active topic, append it to that topic's measurements[] before the session ends. The active topic is named in CLAUDE.md so it is always in context.

measurement_schema.how_measured: the procedure, specific enough to repeat
measurement_schema.source: file path or command that produced it

opening_sequence.why: Most evidence already exists. Zero new build, strongest differentiation.
Natural sequel; the LLM-vs-deterministic split per pipeline is the number.
Forces a build already owed — deterministic-core unit tests are on the engineering-health backlog.

candidate_metrics: traceability pass rate (share of published numbers that resolve to a computed signal)
negative-test catch rate (share of deliberately corrupted values the gate rejects)
scope tightness (period-wide vs declared-scope: how many invented-but-in-range values survive)

measurements.metric: negative-test catch rate — distribution gate, tolerance/ratio policy
measurements.how_measured: 1,000 injected plausible pp/% values (seeded uniform 0.6–80.0) across the 25 nearest-to-flip signals, each scoped to that signal's own declared ground truth; measured the share the gate rejected under two policies — core.traceability.SIBC with ratio grounding vs the new DISTRIBUTION policy without it.

measurements.metric: false-rejection rate on true statements — same gate
measurements.how_measured: Ran the real, uncorrupted watchlist sentence for the 25 nearest-to-flip signals through the same check. The 3 failures were the glued-'pp' extractor backtrack (1.9pp -> 1), fixed by the spaced format; locked by test_real_watchlist_sentences_are_never_falsely_rejected.

measurements.metric: newsletter gate (validate_newsletter.check_doc) catch rate — BEFORE rescoping
measurements.how_measured: analysis/measure_groundedness.py, 200 injections per attack across the 3 live issues (release read x2 + deep read), seed 7. Verified by hand: the gate passed 9.1%, 47.3% and 812.6% injected into a body paragraph, and only rejected a 5-figure absurdity.

measurements.metric: newsletter gate catch rate — AFTER per-block scoping + DISTRIBUTION policy
measurements.how_measured: Same harness, same seed, same issues. Three changes: per-block scope with NO issue-wide fallback; core.traceability.DISTRIBUTION policy (rounding width, no ratio grounding); ground truth includes each value as fmt_value renders it. Rendered .md output byte-identical before and after.

measurements.metric: scope width — values a single paragraph was checked against
measurements.value: 735 (289 distinct) → 0 for undeclared blocks, ~10-35 for declared ones
measurements.how_measured: declared_ground_truth() on a release read's 21 declared signals, before vs after per-block scoping.

measurements.metric: distribution gate catch rate — same harness, for comparison
measurements.how_measured: analysis/measure_groundedness.py --gate distribution. Weaker than the newsletter because a claim's scope includes its full period series and ranges, which prose legitimately references ('16-period range'). Next tightening target.

observations: The +9.99 pp incident (2026-07-21): an invented pair-divergence value PASSED the first negative test because the signal was classified scan-like, pooling a pp gap with two pct rates and deriving cumulative sums. Fixing the classification made the same corruption hard-fail. Lesson: a groundedness check is only as good as the ground-truth shape it compares against.
First newsletter traceability cut was vacuous — period-wide scope let invented numbers pass. Rescoping to declared signals only was the fix. Same lesson as Check 4f's evidence_all rescoping.
Negative testing must target the artifact the validator actually reads. The pair-divergence negative test only worked after editing analysis/rbi_atm_pos/insights.json rather than the web copy.

Loosening a gate can be invisible: the distribution gate initially inherited the card policy (abs_tol=0.25 plus ratio grounding) and passed every negative test I threw at it — not because the text was grounded but because a 29-value pool spawns ~800 candidate ratios and a quarter-point tolerance covers most of the number line worth inventing. Catch rate 41.5%. The same check, scoped to rounding width and with ratio grounding removed, catches 95.7%. A gate that never fails is not evidence of correctness; measure the catch rate.

Groundedness has two error rates, and only measuring one is how you ship a gate that blocks the truth. Tightening tolerance took false rejections from 0 to 3/25 until the formatting quirk behind them was fixed; both numbers belong in the measurement.

The gate guarding the subscriber-facing newsletter caught 0% of invented numbers, and had passed every hand-written negative test since it was built. Hand-written negative tests select for numbers the author already suspects; an injection harness does not. Every gate needs a measured catch rate before it can be described as working.

A defensive default is a hole. When per-block scoping was added, blocks with no declared signals fell back to the issue-wide pool 'so nothing breaks' — that single fallback dropped the in-range catch rate from 91.9% to 42.4%, because the injection site was exactly such a block. Removing the fallback took it to 99.5%. Scope defaults should fail closed.

Tightening surfaced two real bugs that the loose gate had been hiding, both display-format rather than data problems: values rendered for the reader (₹2.9L Cr, 12.0 crore) did not match their stored form, and ratio grounding had been silently accepting them as a/b of other values. The fix — ground the value through the same renderer the issue uses — is exact rather than a tolerance guess.
open_questions: Breadth-based (period-wide) checks let an in-range invented number pass. What is the cheapest scoping that closes this for free prose, not just structured values?
Can groundedness be measured as a single rate across both pipelines, or is it inherently per-check?

candidate_metrics: time from regression introduced to regression caught
grounding_artifacts: analysis/check_signal_freshness.py (Check 2f — deterministic recompute-and-diff, an eval in all but name)

candidate_metrics: verbosity bias (does longer output score higher)
override rate (share of generated items the human changes)
share of overrides that indicate a mechanism bug vs a taste difference
share of published output produced deterministically vs generated, per pipeline
count of cards that moved from LLM to deterministic representation (or back) and why
observations: The split is not a style choice — it tracks whether the output shape is mechanical. Scans and distributions are grounded by construction; scalars need judgment about what the movement means.

candidate_metrics: version-over-version delta on a fixed eval set
count of prompt rules added in response to an observed failure (rule provenance)
observations: Prompt rules here are almost all reactive — 'no invented seasonality' and the traceability rules each trace to a specific observed failure. That provenance is the interesting content, not the prompt text.

candidate_metrics: cost of retries as share of total
share of payload the model actually uses
grounding_artifacts: analysis/signals/query.py (builds the evaluate payload — full period series + scan distribution)

candidate_metrics: share of variance that survives the gate
when an agent beat a pipeline and when it did not
share of answers grounded in retrieved context
wall-clock for a full gate run
blast radius when the model is wrong
share of failures caught before publication
grounding_artifacts: analysis/pipelines/atm_pos/generate_atm_pos_insights.py (falls back to deterministic when no eval exists)

topics.topic: Unit economics of an LLM feature
candidate_metrics: share of pipeline cost that is LLM

observations: This project already runs an informal routing policy: Opus for run/render work, Fable for mechanism work. Whether that policy is right is measurable.

candidate_metrics: share of improvements traceable to observed usage
time to revalidate after a model change
